_readme.description: Qualitative player notes for LDB 2026 auction. Merged into ldb_data.js at build time.

fields.tags: Array of tag strings. Built-in recognized tags: INJURED, IL, DTD, SLEEPER, BREAKOUT, BUST, CLOSER_RISK, ROLE_UNCLEAR, HANDCUFF, PLATOON, PROSPECT, AGING, BOUNCE_BACK, STREAKY, DEEP_LEAGUE, ADP_VALUE, ADP_AVOID, ROFR_TARGET, LDB_NEED, SP_LOCKED, IL_START, DELAYED
fields.note: Free-text scouting note shown in player card tooltip / intel section
fields.health_pct: 0-100 estimated availability. 100 = full season, 70 = ~112 games / 115 IP expected

_readme.auto_tags_from_stats: generate_data.py also auto-applies stat-based tags. Manual tags here take precedence / stack on top.
_readme.name_matching: Use exact projection file names. Fuzzy matching is applied as fallback.
_readme.manual_notes: UI-added notes (object, normalized-name keys). Overrides players[].note. Added/edited via Player Card.

players.name: Ronald Acuña Jr.
players.note: FRY keeper (K2). Full ACL recovery expected. If healthy, top-3 overall — OPS+OBP+aSB monster.

players.name: Corbin Carroll
players.note: Hamate surgery (right hand, mid-Feb). PL dropped only ~2 spots — minimal long-term power impact per data. Avg 26 HR / 40 SB / 114 R over 3 full seasons. Brief IL start expected but Diamondbacks not rushing. Top-10 upside when healthy.
players.role: ARI OF — hamate surgery, brief IL start, ETA April

players.name: Drew Rasmussen
players.note: FRY keeper (K3/ROFR). Confirmed Rays SP2. Healthy spring — locked into rotation. ROFR activates at auction.

players.name: Matthew Liberatore
players.note: FRY keeper (H2). Cardinals SP1 — confirmed rotation anchor. Improved command in 2024, poised for breakout.

players.name: Hunter Greene
players.note: FRY ROFR. ELBOW PAIN — shut down from throwing, IL to open season. Timeline unknown. Do not overpay.

players.name: José Alvarado
players.note: FRY ROFR. Elite stuff but walk rate and blown save history create real VIJAY variance.

players.name: Tanner Scott
players.note: FRY ROFR. Dodgers closer situation. Good K rate, reliable holds even if not locking saves.

players.name: Tyler O'Neill
players.note: FRY ROFR. Power/speed upside but injury history and contact issues cap floor.

players.name: Shohei Ohtani
players.note: Listed as Dodgers SP2 — pitching return confirmed for 2026. Also DH. Dual-threat elite value: OPS monster at plate + frontline SP innings. Top-1 overall if fully healthy pitching.

players.name: Gerrit Cole
players.note: Out for entire 2026 season. Missed all of 2025 after TJ surgery. DO NOT DRAFT.

players.name: Carlos Rodón
players.note: Yankees note confirms out until May/June alongside Cole. Back-half value only. Yankees rotation locked without him (Fried, Schlittler, Weathers, Warren, Gil).

players.name: Yoshinobu Yamamoto
players.note: Listed as Dodgers SP1 — TJ return confirmed, opening day starter. If stuff is back, elite upside. Huge bounce-back candidate if velocity holds.

players.name: Spencer Strider
players.note: Confirmed Braves SP2. TJ return — opening day rotation. Massive upside if velocity is back. Risk is real but reward is top-5 SP ceiling.

players.name: Bryce Miller
players.note: PRP injection, left side inflammation. High chance of IL to open season — Hancock vs. Criswell competing for Mariners SP5 in his absence. Spring return unclear.

players.name: Jordan Wicks
players.note: IL to open 2026 with left arm nerve issue. Was finally going to get his rotation shot — now delayed further. Cubs rotation: Imanaga, Boyd, Horton, Taillon, Cabrera locked.

players.name: Joey Wentz
players.note: Carted off field covering first base (right leg). Walked to clubhouse but severity unclear. Out of Braves rotation — Elder gets SP5. Spring line: 0.2 IP, 1 ER.

players.name: Corbin Burnes
players.note: Diamondbacks note: not back until July/August. Half-season value. Back of rotation arms starting for ARI until return.

players.name: Tarik Skubal
players.note: Tigers SP1 — locked. Reigning AL Cy Young, elite ratios. Only risk is modest regression. Premier auction target.

players.name: Framber Valdez
players.note: Tigers SP2 — confirmed. Elite groundball rate suppresses HRA — ideal for LDB. Durable workhorse. MGS consistently strong.

players.name: Paul Skenes
players.note: Pirates SP1 — confirmed. Best stuff in baseball. Only question is IP ceiling in year 2. Premium target.

players.name: Chris Sale
players.note: Braves SP1 — confirmed. Reigning NL Cy Young at 35. History of TJ/rib injuries. Monitor workload all season.

players.name: Max Fried
players.note: Yankees SP1 — locked. Dominant NL arm, now in AL. Command-first profile suits LDB ratios well. Full season starter.

players.name: Logan Gilbert
players.note: Mariners SP1 — locked. Elite K rate, low HR/9, durability. Bryce Miller's uncertainty only elevates his value as team anchor.

players.name: Dylan Cease
players.note: Blue Jays SP2 — locked. High-K but HR/9 and walk rate make ratios volatile. Petco gone — Rogers Centre is hitter-friendly. Discount from ADP.

players.name: Zack Wheeler
players.note: Phillies confirmed. Elite workhorse, consistent MGS producer. One of safest SP floor plays in the pool.

players.name: Tanner Bibee
players.note: Guardians SP1 — confirmed. Breakout 2024, young and durable. Strong K rate with good ratios in pitcher-friendly Cleveland.

players.name: Joe Ryan
players.note: Twins SP1 — confirmed. PabLó out for the year opens innings. Durable strikeout arm, underrated LDB value.

players.name: Kevin Gausman
players.note: Spring: 3.1 IP, 0 ER, 3 H, 0 BB, 3 K. Confirmed Blue Jays SP1. Velo 2-3 ticks down at 92+ mph but scout says normal spring fluctuation — don't worry. Splitter getting whiffs, keeping it down. All good.

players.name: Sandy Alcantara
players.note: Marlins SP1 — confirmed. Return from TJ surgery. If fully healthy, workhorse with elite ground ball rate and MGS upside.

players.name: Jesús Luzardo
players.note: Phillies SP2 — confirmed. Excellent stuff, finally healthy and in a great park. Premium K upside.

players.name: Cristopher Sánchez
players.note: Phillies SP1 — confirmed. Groundball machine in a great park. Deep outings, solid LDB ratios.

players.name: Aaron Nola
players.note: Phillies SP3 — confirmed. Veteran workhorse, consistent innings and ratios. Solid mid-tier LDB floor.

players.name: Shota Imanaga
players.note: Cubs SP1 — confirmed. Elite command, plus K rate. Wicks on IL only strengthens his team role. High-floor LDB value.

players.name: Hunter Brown
players.note: Spring: 4 IP, 0 ER, 0 H, 2 BB, 9 K. Dominant. Sitting 97 mph, gaining +3.7" movement on four-seamer and +1.5" on sinker. Changeup thrown for strikes. True ace performance. Astros SP1 locked.

players.name: Cole Ragans
players.note: Royals SP1 — locked. Best stuff on a weak staff. Breakout candidate — K rate and control improving.

players.name: Luis Gil
players.note: Yankees SP5 — locked (Cole & Rodón out until May/June). Gets a full slate of starts early. Elevated value during injury window.

players.name: Jacob deGrom
players.note: Rangers SP1 — confirmed. History of IL stints but listed as opening day starter. Elite stuff when healthy, massive upside with massive risk.

players.name: Shane Bieber
players.note: TJ return — not in confirmed Guardians rotation (Bibee, Williams, Cecconi, Cantillo, Allen listed). May return mid-season.

players.name: Zack Littell
players.note: Signed with Nationals SP5. Nationals love 90+ pitch outings — innings-eater upside in a weak rotation. Deep league value for MGS accumulation.

players.name: Edwin Díaz
players.note: Signed 3-year $69M deal with the Dodgers. Now LAD closer — elite stuff, locked role. Removes the Mets save situation uncertainty.

players.name: Jhoan Duran
players.note: Best non-closer RP in baseball. Splitter is a weapon. High-leverage lockdown, holds accumulator.

players.name: Andrés Muñoz
players.note: Mariners closer — elite K rate. Low BS history. One of the cleanest VIJAY profiles available.

players.name: Blake Snell
players.note: 2x Cy Young winner but extreme durability risk. Worth drafting/stashing in dynasty/keeper. Per spring 2026 news, health is uncertain again — draft at your own risk.

players.name: Bryce Elder
players.note: Gets Braves SP5 by default after Wentz injury. Spring: 3.1 IP, 5 ER, 6 H, 3 BB — ugly. Command is a mess. Only here because rotation is thin. Low floor.

players.name: Justin Wrobleski
players.note: Spring: 3 IP, 0 ER, 2 H, 0 BB, 3 K (43 pitches). Scout: 'How do you not let him start instead of Sasaki? He's clearly better.' SLEEPER ALERT — Dodgers SP5 is his to lose.
players.role: Dodgers SP5 — SLEEPER, better than Sasaki

players.name: Logan Gillaspie
players.note: Spring: 4 IP, 0 ER, 2 H, 2 BB, 2 K. Scout: 'I think he might be Padres SP5 — sorry Márquez and Buehler.' Great changeup feel, death ball curve, sinker inside to RHB. Kolek comp. Needs slider/sweeper feel vs RHB but cutter to LHB is there. Sleeper with floor.
players.role: Padres SP5 — likely locked, Kolek comp

players.name: Landen Roupp
players.note: Spring: 3 IP, 1 ER, 2 H, 1 BB, 2 K. 91 mph cutter + changeup to LHB, sinker/curve base. Needs to get sinker inside to RHB (trying too hard to emulate Nola's backdoor). Leading Tidwell for Giants SP5. 'A few impressive strikeout games when curve and change are cooking.'

players.name: Blade Tidwell
players.note: Spring: 1.2 IP, 4 ER, 5 H, 2 BB, 4 K (53 pitches — was in contention). Lost vert on four-seamer, can't find zone vs RHB. Looked better vs LHB. Clearly behind Roupp. 'Fun mid-season pickup.'
players.role: Giants — behind Roupp for SP5, mid-season watch

players.name: Jacob Misiorowski
players.note: Spring: 3.1 IP, 1 ER, 2 H, 1 BB, 6 K. Called 'aces gonna ace.' Elite stuff but command is wonky — expect 5 innings standard, rarely 6+. High-K ceiling but walk rate will hurt ratios and MGS.

players.name: George Kirby
players.note: Spring: 3 IP, 1 ER, 2 H, 1 BB, 2 K. Testing cutters + more sinkers. Slider at 84-87 mph with -10/-10" two-plane break described as 'stupid good.' When he locates it — game over. Mariners SP2 locked.

players.name: Nathan Eovaldi
players.note: Spring: 5 IP, 2 ER, 5 H, 1 BB, 4 K. Healthy, sitting firm 95 mph (tick up from last year). Testing slider with some solid reps. Rangers SP2 confirmed. Moving up ranks per scouts.

players.name: Kyle Harrison
players.note: Spring: 3 IP, 1 ER, 2 H, 2 BB, 8 K. Sitting 95 mph at 60th pitch. Changeup has elite depth (3/11 whiffs to RHB). Added sweeper and curve. Command still developing but upside is real. Brewers SP2.

players.name: Joey Cantillo
players.note: Spring: 4.2 IP, 2 ER, 5 H, 0 BB, 4 K. Changeup is filthy (9/19 whiffs, 8/13 vs RHB alone). Textbook HIPSTER — dominant vs RHB-heavy lineups, exposed vs LHB. Four-seamer and slider lack feel. Guardians SP5.

players.name: Simeon Woods Richardson
players.note: Spring: 4 IP, 1 ER, 1 H, 0 BB, 5 K. Velocity still down at 92 mph — concerning. Splitter is excellent (steep release, used vs both sides) but slider was all over. 5 K came against poor lineup. Don't trust until velo climbs.

players.name: Andre Pallante
players.note: Spring: 4 IP, 1 ER, 2 H, 1 BB, 3 K. Sinker inside to RHB, cut-fastball to LHB. 44% slider usage to RHB — moved away from four-seamer. Cardinals going with him. Could be decent in a stealth way.

players.name: Jameson Taillon
players.note: Spring: 3.2 IP, 1 ER, 2 H, 2 BB, 3 K. Velocity down to just 91 mph — rough. Cut action not showing the jump scouts wanted. Cubs SP4 by default but not inspiring. 'Not there yet' per scouts.

players.name: Ryan Weathers
players.note: Spring: 2 IP, 5 ER, 7 H, 2 BB, 3 K (line ugly but context matters — relieved twice). Scout says don't change opinion — sinker wrinkles + changeup had less drop, but he's still great. Yankees SP3 locked.

players.name: Taijuan Walker
players.note: Spring: 3.1 IP, 0 ER, 0 H, 2 BB, 3 K. Finally using cutter (20% usage, 77% strikes) — his best pitch. In Phillies rotation if cutter keeps cooking. Some NL-only upside per scouts. Phillies SP4.
players.role: Phillies SP4 — locked if cutter holds

players.name: Dean Kremer
players.note: Spring (WBC): 4.1 IP, 0 ER, 2 H, 1 BB, 4 K. Cutter cooking (6/18 whiffs). If Orioles go six-man he's in the rotation. Bassitt signed — Wells out — Kremer next in line.

players.name: Luis Severino
players.note: Spring (WBC/DOM): 4 IP, 1 ER, 3 H, 0 BB, 5 K at 98 mph — likely amped for country. In-season expect 96 mph. Sweeper + cutter is his life now. Sacramento home park is brutal. Fun streamer, not a core hold.

players.name: Chase Burns
players.note: Spring: 3.2 IP, 4 ER, 4 H, 1 BB, 4 K. Threw 20% changeups to LHB — getting strikes but no whiffs yet. Would get whiffs with better down-and-away location. Reds SP3 locked. Developing arsenal.

players.name: Aaron Civale
players.note: Spring: 3.2 IP, 2 ER, 5 H, 0 BB, 4 K. 'That's Civale' — everything worse than 2024. 5.5 ft extension helps slightly but Sacramento is a brutal home park. Hard avoid.

players.name: Jacob Lopez
players.note: Spring: 2.0 IP, 3 ER, 3 H, 1 BB, 1 K. Sinker not armside, changeup non-competitive vs RHB. Experimenting with cutter/slider/sweeper — unclear identity. Need another look before trusting.

players.name: Tyler Wells
players.note: NOT in Orioles rotation — Bassitt signed. Moves to bullpen or depth role. Was competing for rotation spot but that door is closed.

players.name: Jake Bennett
players.note: Spring: 1.2 IP, 2 ER, 2 H, 0 BB, 2 K. Intriguing lefty — 7.1 ft extension at 93/94 mph, cutter inside to RHB (more gyro slider at 85/86). Changeup can be good but needs more usage. Struggled getting -21" iHB sinker inside to LHB. Developing but the raw tools are there.

players.name: Brady Singer
players.note: Spring: 3.0 IP, 0 ER, 2 H, 2 BB, 3 K. Only faced RHB (sinker/slider day). Velo a tick down, less rise on four-seamer. Interesting new 81 mph cutter — possibly a bad sweeper, possibly a new weapon. Nothing alarming. Reds SP4 confirmed.

players.name: Chase Petty
players.note: Spring: 2.0 IP, 0 ER, 2 H, 1 BB, 1 K — removed from spring squad after this. Up to 97/98 mph but Empty Velocity, some four-seamers acting as sinkers with poor movement. Can't find the zone. Splitter spotted in there. Monitor mid-season.

players.name: Sean Newcomb
players.note: Spring: 3.0 IP, 2 ER, 3 H, 0 BB, 2 K. Doing his best to force a White Sox rotation spot — not going to happen per scouts. Likely bullpen.
players.role: White Sox — unlikely to crack rotation

players.name: Noah Cameron
players.note: Spring: 3.0 IP, 2 ER, 3 H, 1 BB, 3 K. Sitting 92+ mph with less cut action than expected — slight concern. Cutter inside to RHB not as sharp. BUT 6/19 four-seamer whiffs keeping it upstairs is encouraging. Scout still in on him — ranked near Boyd/Eflin tier, past IL stashes. Royals SP5.

players.name: Jack Kochanowicz
players.note: Spring: 4.0 IP, 1 ER, 3 H, 0 BB, 2 K. Lost sink, can't get it inside to RHB — core problem. Slider improved with more drop (-03" at 88 mph), but also can't locate it. Mixed signals. Not inspiring.

players.name: Braxton Garrett
players.note: Spring: 3.0 IP, 1 ER, 1 H, 1 BB, 2 K. Returning from injury — cutter acts like 88 mph gyro slider, 83 mph slider is two-plane sweeper (-11 sweep, -5 depth). Four-seamer has cut action. Not jamming RHB enough, command needs help. Skills are visible — could find groove as season progresses. Marlins SP2.

players.name: Freddy Peralta
players.note: Spring: 3.0 IP, 2 ER, 3 H, 1 BB, 4 K. BIG change — far fewer sliders, leaning changeup/curve more vs RHB. Curve at 78 mph with mediocre movement (1-2" less drop/sweep). Changeup improved (less velo, more movement). RED FLAG: extension dropped massively to 6.5 ft on four-seamer, 6.2 ft on changeup. Scout calls it spring intensity issue — must monitor. Mets SP2.

players.name: Alan Rangel
players.note: Spring: 3.0 IP, 3 ER, 6 H, 0 BB, 3 K. 94 mph, 40 pitches, shaky command, nothing stellar except high vert on four-seamer (angle-driven). Not beating Walker or Painter for PHI rotation spot. Scout says 'SIGN GIOLITO ALREADY.'

players.name: Carmen Mlodzinski
players.note: Spring: 3.0 IP, 1 ER, 5 H, 0 BB, 4 K. 58 pitches — filthy 83/84 mph curveball, splitter with 3/13 whiffs. Sinker down from 96 to 94/95 and sitting away to RHB — can't get it armside. Ideal profile is sinker/curve with splitter/four-seamer vs LHB, but command isn't there yet. Competing for Pirates SP5.

players.name: J.P. France
players.note: Spring (WBC): 1.1 IP, 3 ER, 3 H, 1 BB, 1 K. One phenomenal cutter, rest was meh. Not in Astros rotation plans.

players.name: Ryan Gusto
players.note: Spring: 3.0 IP, 0 ER, 1 H, 1 BB, 2 K. Lots of vert, can't elevate four-seamer. Kitchen-sink approach but command isn't there. Behind Garrett for Marlins rotation spot.

players.name: Cody Ponce
players.note: Spring: 3.0 IP, 0 ER, 0 H, 0 BB, 1 K (cruised through, probably threw more after). 36 pitches — only faced one LHB who saw a splitter and grounded out. Blue Jays depth arm.

players.name: Francisco Lindor
players.note: Hamate surgery + elbow cleanup this offseason. Back in Cactus League. Hard-hit rate key metric to watch — may suppress power early. 30/30 floor when right. Top SS in OBP+OPS formats.
players.role: NYM SS — back in spring lineup, monitor hard-hit rate

players.name: Sal Stewart
players.note: [PL #118 — Nate ALL IN] PLV 600 PA projection: .272 BA / 31 HR / 21st overall. Immediate power in 18 MLB games. 1B+3B eligible. Gavin Lux trade = full-time role. Strong hit tool. Best late-round power stash in draft.
players.role: CIN 1B/3B — likely starter after Lux trade

players.name: Konnor Griffin
players.note: [PL #144] Best prospect in baseball. Power + speed + maturing hit tool. <100 MLB G likely in 2026. Dynasty gold — cheap in auction. PIT OF.

players.name: JJ Wetherholt
players.note: [PL #179 — Nate: high floor] STL starter, 17-20 HR / 15-20 SB / high BA. 2B eligible April. Per Nate: highest floor among 2026 prospects. Cheap in standard auctions.

players.name: Carter Jensen
players.note: [PL #235 — Nate bullish] 166 wRC+ / 20 HR in Triple-A. 20 HR / 10 SB upside per Nate. ~100 G behind Perez. Excellent value late for C slot.

players.name: Jakob Marsee
players.note: [PL #71 — Nate likes] 189 wRC+ in August debut. Swing change added ~3 mph exit velo. Bat-to-ball elite, 40 SB ceiling. Power skepticism (never slugged .428+ in minors). OBP league value. MIA leadoff.

players.name: Sal Frelick
players.note: [PL dropped 50 spots from Oct to #170] PL: 2025 was 90th percentile outcome. Likely returns to bottom-third of order, fewer PA. Not an LDB target — speed without OBP/power is low value in this format.
players.role: MIL OF — platoon risk, order drop

players.name: Michael Harris II
players.note: [PL #58, jumped +13] 14 HR / .302 AVG from July 11 on. Back-to-back 3-month slumps BUT 3rd-youngest regular in NL. Profar loss means better lineup spot. True 20-20 upside when he's on, brutal holds when he's off.

players.name: James Wood
players.note: [PL #32, jumped +8] Top-10 ceiling per PL. Issue: passive against breakers in zone. Not an easy fix, but WSN OBP format + elite raw power = LDB value in OBP+OPS. Cheap if passive ST keeps price down.

players.name: Colson Montgomery
players.note: [PL #95] 32 HR via extreme pull approach. Sophomore regression risk real — contact below average, decision value below average. Projections confirm power upside. All-or-nothing: 30 HR + poor ratios OR busted sophomore.

players.name: Roman Anthony
players.note: [PL #61] 22-25 HR / 10-12 SB / 100 R ceiling in 2026. BOS leadoff. Growth in power legitimate per stats. Projections more conservative (18-19 HR, 85-90 R). Still a top OBP+OPS play at right price.

players.name: Wyatt Langford
players.note: [PL #39] Top-3 prospect tools. 2 months wRC+160+, 2 months wRC+75 — extreme variance. Improved walk rate signals consistency coming. Plus power + speed (24yo). LDB: if price drops due to inconsistency, pounce.

players.name: Hunter Goodman
players.note: [PL #41] 144 G in 2026 — rare Coors player who produced at home AND road (18 road HR). .300+ road BA. Catcher + DH eligible. Strong LDB value: power + OBP in Coors = HR + OPS category hitter.

players.name: Agustín Ramírez
players.note: [PL #103] 21 HR / 16 SB / 136 G as rookie. Poor ratios (.231/.287) but volume + speed combo. LDB: aSB + HR value. 150 G upside in 2026. Youth (22yo) + MIA lineup spot.

players.name: Drake Baldwin
players.note: [PL #75] 19 HR / 15.2% K / .274 BA as rookie. PL: repeat 2025 is a great outcome. Top-6 C if healthy. Ozuna departure = more PA. ATL lineup is strong context.
players.role: ATL C — full-time with Ozuna gone

players.name: Brice Turang
players.note: [PL #34] Power breakout: 7 HR in 2024 → 18 HR in 2025. 10 in August alone. 26yo — may stick. 15 HR / 25-30 SB / high BA / MIL 2B. LDB: speed + OBP + power emerging = strong 2B target.

players.name: Luis Robert Jr.
players.note: [PL #90] 14 HR / 33 SB in 110 G — projected 20 HR / 50 SB pace. Final month: sub-20% K rate. NYM gamble. LDB: aSB ceiling is enormous if healthy. Floor: brutal injury + cold stretches. Boom/bust.
players.role: NYM OF — new team, healthcare question

players.name: Chandler Simpson
players.note: [PL #117] Zero barrels in 376 batted-ball events. But 44 SB in 104 G — on pace for 70+ in full season. TBR platoon limits AB. LDB: aSB efficiency could be spectacular if usage rate holds. Pure speed play.

players.name: Shea Langeliers
players.note: [PL #42] 17 HR in 36 games post-ASB (!). Full season: 25-30 HR. K rate improved 7.5 pts. Big second-half variance — PL cautions against assuming 36-game pace, but skills are real. Premium C target.

players.name: Rafael Devers
players.note: Back in Cactus League after brief hamstring tightness — should be fine for Opening Day. Zone-contact concerns remain (73.8% — worst among 500+ PA qualifiers). LDB: OPS/OBP value real but contact erosion is ongoing risk.

players.name: Byron Buxton
players.note: [PL #46, dropped 7] First 125+ G season since 2017. PL caveats: if you've taken other injury risks (Acuña, Alvarez, Seager) — don't add Buxton. Lineup also weak. Strong upside but high-risk stack.

players.name: Manny Machado
players.note: [PL #28, dropped 5] Lineup concern: SDP offense is thin beyond Machado. Laureano regression risk leaves 3-hitter deep lineup. PL: found himself talking out of Machado in actual drafts. Counting stat ceiling capped.

players.name: Nick Kurtz
players.note: [PL #12] Finished top-30 in just 117 G as rookie — 5th-best 1B. Strikeout rate spikes but hot streaks are elite. Sophomore slump risk. Minor league bandbox context. True breakout if K rate stabilizes.

players.name: Ketel Marte
players.note: [PL #24, big jump from Oct] Multiple projection systems like Marte for big 2026. 25+ HR / .280+ BA / top 2B. ARI lineup concerns in second half. Injury risk (foot, hand in 2025) but floor is strong.

players.name: Ceddanne Rafaela
players.note: [PL #79] Elite first-half: 14 HR / 13 SB / .271. Second half: 2 HR, power fell off cliff vs fastballs. Key question: can he punish fastballs? If yes = top-75 hitter. If no = huge negative floor.

players.name: Geraldo Perdomo
players.note: [PL #54] PL: overcounted him in October. Counting stats come down hard: 90 R / 100 RBI not repeatable, more like 90 R / 60 RBI. Speed + OBP are real. LDB: OBP value but aRBI capped. ADP likely too high.

players.name: Garrett Crochet
players.note: PL's #1 overall SP. BOS SP1. Elite K rate (12+ K/9), now adding more pitch mix. Workload management concern — only 3rd full SP season. MGS ceiling is real if they let him throw 200 IP. Top target.

players.name: Bryan Woo
players.note: PL #4 AGA1. SEA SP1. Elite stuff — 96 mph sinker + plus slider + developing change. IP ceiling the only knock — Mariners cautious. If he hits 160+ IP, elite MGS + K combo. Buy early.

players.name: Eury Perez
players.note: PL #21 AGA Holly. MIA SP. Young flamethrower returning from TJ. 6'8" RHP with elite extension, mid-90s heat + filthy curve. Marlins will manage innings carefully. Elite ceiling if healthy.

players.name: Kyle Bradish
players.note: PL #20 AGA Holly. BAL SP. TJ surgery in 2024, full return for 2026. Strong command-based arsenal — plus slider, curveball, changeup. If fully healthy, elite ratios in Camden (good GB park).

players.name: Ryan Pepiot
players.note: PL #23 AGA Holly. TBR SP2. Low-90s heat elevated by exceptional extension and deception. Elite groundball profile. Park + defense suit him perfectly. K rate should stay north of 25%.

players.name: Cam Schlittler
players.note: PL #25 AGA Holly. NYY SP. Young power arm in a loaded rotation — gets real innings with Cole/Rodón out until May/June. 96+ mph + elite breaking ball. Breakout candidate in late auction.

players.name: Ranger Suarez
players.note: PL #55 Toby Upside 1. Now with BOS. Command-heavy LHP with elite WHIP profile. Low K rate but exceptional contact management — BABIP suppressor. BOS park fine for LHPs. Ratios specialist.

players.name: Trevor Rogers
players.note: PL #32 Holly. BAL SP. Traded from MIA. Camden Yards is a big upgrade over Marlins Park for a LHP. Plus fastball and slider combo, was an All-Star in 2021. Low-cost bounce-back target.

players.name: Tatsuya Imai
players.note: PL #33 Holly. HOU SP. NPB import — posted from Japan with exceptional K rate. Elite splitter generates tons of weak contact. HOU defense elite. ADP undervalues him; buy at discount.

players.name: Nolan McLean
players.note: PL #34 Holly. NYM SP. Young power arm — 96+ mph heat and a bat-missing slider. NYM rotation spot secured. Full season could produce 160+ K. Late-auction value if overlooked.

players.name: Bubba Chandler
players.note: PL #35 Holly. PIT SP. Former two-sport athlete (QB at Clemson) with elite raw stuff — mid-90s + hammer curve. 2025 was his development year; 2026 is breakout season. Buy cheap.

players.name: Trey Yesavage
players.note: PL #36 Holly. TOR SP. Advanced command for young age — four-pitch mix with two above-average off-speed. TOR got him in trade, slotted into rotation. Underrated in standard rankings.

players.name: Cade Horton
players.note: PL #37 Holly. CHC SP. Highly anticipated debut arm — mid-to-high 90s + three sharp secondary pitches. Full-season opportunity in CHC rotation. K upside + Wrigley day games = variance.

players.name: Robbie Ray
players.note: PL #38 Cherry Bomb But Moreso. SFG SP. Two years of TJ recovery — fully healthy and re-signed. When right, elite K rate (12+ K/9) + solid walk rate. Oracle Park plays well. High upside at discount.

players.name: Kris Bubic
players.note: PL #43 Cherry Bomb But Moreso. KCR SP. LHP with above-average changeup and deceptive mechanics — induces weak contact at elite rate. Low-K but exceptional WHIP profile. Undervalued in ERA-heavy formats.

players.name: Edward Cabrera
players.note: PL #46 Holly I Feel A Little Worse About. CHC SP (via trade). Electric stuff — 99 mph + 87 mph splitter. Health history the concern; when right, elite K rate. CHC getting him in rotation.

players.name: MacKenzie Gore
players.note: PL #50 Frizzle. TEX SP. Recovering from elbow issues. When fully healthy, one of the better LHPs in the league — K rate north of 27%, elite slider. Innings cap likely but fantasy value real.

players.name: Emmet Sheehan
players.note: PL #51 Frizzle. LAD SP. Returning from TJ surgery — big fastball + elite breaking ball. LAD innings management means 100-130 IP ceiling, but K/9 should be elite. Buy cheap, stash.

players.name: Nick Lodolo
players.note: PL #53 Toby Upside 1. CIN SP. Tall LHP with premium extension and elite changeup. Health concerns in 2024-25 damped value — when right, 25%+ K rate + top-tier WHIP. CIN isn't a pitcher's park but ratios hold.

players.name: Gavin Williams
players.note: PL #57 Toby Upside 1. CLE SP. 97 mph sinker + cutter combo generates elite GB rate. CLE defense + park makes him a ratios monster. K rate tick-up in 2025 signals approach maturation.

players.name: Shane Baz
players.note: PL #58 Toby Upside 1. BAL SP (via trade). Electric 97 mph four-seamer + plus breaking ball. Multiple IL stints in 2024-25. When healthy, legit top-20 SP upside. Buy at injury discount.

players.name: Spencer Schwellenbach
players.note: Bone spur removal surgery (same procedure as Waldrep). Expected out first ~2 months. 3.09 ERA through June 2025 before fractured elbow ended season. Worth stashing in deeper leagues — elite stuff when healthy.
players.role: ATL SP — bone spur surgery, ETA June 2026

players.name: Jared Jones
players.note: PL #66 Injury Stash 1. PIT SP. Top-of-rotation stuff — mid-90s four-seamer + elite slider. Oblique strain cut 2025 short. When healthy, elite K rate + legit MGS ceiling. Stash at right price.

players.name: Logan Henderson
players.note: PL #75 HIPSTER 1. MIL SP. Young arm with advanced four-pitch mix — plus fastball, curve, change, cutter. PL's HIPSTER picks are contrarian value calls. Henderson is the highest-upside arm in that group.

players.name: Robby Snelling
players.note: PL #76 HIPSTER 1. MIA SP. LHP with above-average stuff for 21-year-old — low-90s but exceptional command + movement. Marlins building around him. Innings cap in play but buy cheap for the ceiling.

players.name: Braxton Ashcraft
players.note: PL #77 HIPSTER 1. PIT SP. Tall RHP with 96 mph sinker generating extreme groundball rates (60%+). Elite contact-management profile. PIT defense is solid. K rate modest but ratios (ERA/WHIP) should shine.

players.name: Kodai Senga
players.note: PL #80 Toby Upside 3. NYM SP. Ghost fork is one of the best pitches in baseball. Calf/shoulder issues wiped out 2025. Full-season return is huge — if healthy, elite K rate + Mets rotation anchor.

players.name: Bailey Ober
players.note: PL #82 Toby Upside 3. MIN SP. 6'9" frame with extreme downward angle — K rate consistently 26%+ despite modest velo. Plus slider + changeup. MIN park OK for pitchers. Perennial underdog value.

players.name: Jack Leiter
players.note: PL #92 Late Upside. TEX SP. Former #2 overall pick finally getting full rotation shot. Plus fastball + elite cutter combo. TEX park challenging but K upside real. Could be 2026 breakout arm.

players.name: Grayson Rodriguez
players.note: PL #97 Late Upside. Now LAA SP. Former top BAL prospect — traded to Angels. 97 mph + excellent secondaries. 2025 inconsistency was approach-based, not stuff. Change of scenery could unlock.

players.name: Roki Sasaki
players.note: PL #103 Possible Job Upside. LAD. Elite splitter is historically dominant. Still unclear if he's in LAD rotation vs bullpen bridge role. Wait for clarity — if SP role confirmed, instant top-40 arm.

players.name: Jackson Jobe
players.note: PL #153 Injury Stash 2. DET SP. One of the best pitching prospects in baseball — mid-90s + elite slider + change. Health setback in 2025, returning in 2026. DET being careful. AA-eligible if available.

players.name: Quinn Mathews
players.note: PL #170 Spec Add 2026 Prospect. STL SP. LHP with above-average command and developing breaking ball. Cardinals love him as rotation depth. Could see meaningful innings in 2026. Spec add only.

players.name: Pablo López
players.note: Torn UCL at start of spring training. Out for entire 2026 season. Posted 2.74 ERA over 14 starts in 2025. Dynasty stash only.
players.role: MIN SP — torn UCL, out full 2026 season

players.name: Felix Bautista
players.note: Labrum + rotator cuff surgery. Missed 2024 with TJ, saved 19 in 2025 before shoulder issues. Might not pitch at all in 2026. Ryan Helsley now closer in BAL.
players.role: BAL RP — labrum+RC surgery, likely out full 2026

players.name: Josh Hader
players.note: Left biceps inflammation has kept him off the mound all spring — hasn't progressed past flat-ground work. Follows 2025 shoulder strain (team says unrelated). Unlikely ready for Opening Day. Bryan Abreu covering closer role to start.
players.role: HOU RP — biceps inflammation, Opening Day IL likely

players.name: Ryan Helsley
players.note: Added to Baltimore to close after Felix Bautista's surgery. Top-tier closer in his own right. Locked role in BAL.

players.name: Bryan Abreu
players.note: Houston will not rush Hader (biceps) — Abreu is the closer to start 2026. High-leverage arm with closer stuff. Roster immediately.
players.role: HOU RP — closer to open season (Hader out)

players.name: Carlos Estévez
players.note: Average fastball velocity down ~6 mph from 2025 this spring. Still projects for 20-25 saves but ERA likely 3.50-4.00 range. Monitor velocity trend.
players.role: RP — velocity down, closer situation to watch

players.name: J.P. Crawford
players.note: Missed brief time with shoulder pain but back in Cactus League. Said he's played through worse. Barring setback, fine for Opening Day.
players.role: SEA SS — shoulder rest, expected Opening Day ready
